pattern: At every position there are only two possible values to place, so tracking the best run length achievable while ending on each of those two choices is enough to build up the answer left to right.
hints: At each index you only ever have two possible values to place, so track two running lengths: the best non-decreasing run ending here if you picked from the first array, and the best if you picked from the second array.

A run ending at this index with a given choice can only extend a run from the previous index if the previous index's chosen value is no greater than this index's chosen value -- check both of the previous index's choices.
Reset a run to length one whenever neither previous choice is small enough to extend it, and keep a running maximum over every run length seen so far.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, first array alone is best
single element, trivially a run of length one
both arrays strictly decreasing on their own
arrays that must alternate choice at every step to stay non-decreasing

stress.description: random values at every position across the full allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums1 = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums1, nums2]
